title: Logarithmic multiplication count of square-and-multiply
statement_informal: Square-and-multiply exponentiation uses at most 2 times the number of bits of the exponent multiplications (logarithmic in the exponent).

cost_model.notes: Exponent given as a binary digit list; cost bounded by 2 * length (= 2*(floor(log2 e)+1)). The Rocq target also proves the value-correctness companion square_and_multiply_correct.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 2, evaluation of powers (fact only)
